title: Delicate Dutch Corn Chowder
description: A bright citrusy chowder with a glassy, jelly-like texture, simmered in a mason jar for quick weeknight meals.
instructions: In a small saucepan, combine 1 tsp agar-agar powder with 1 cup coconut milk and whisk until fully dissolved; set aside to thicken.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a separate pot over medium heat and sauté diced onion and minced garlic until translucent, about 2 minutes.
Add fresh and frozen corn kernels to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring to coat with the oil.
Stir in 1 cup tomato confit, smoked paprika, sea salt, and black pepper, cooking for another 1 minute.
Pour in the remaining coconut milk along with the dissolved agar-agar mixture and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, uncovered, for 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the agar-agar to set into a glassy consistency.
Taste and adjust seasoning, adding more salt or lime juice if desired.
Transfer the chowder into mason jars, leaving a small headspace at the top.
Garnish with fresh cilantro if desired and serve immediately, or chill for 1 hour to allow the jelly-like texture to firm.
